input.background:
$\mathrm{NO}_2$ concentrations (in parts per billion) in the exhaust fumes of different types of vehicle (cars, minibuses, and buses) were sampled. The following data were obtained: 

Car: 69, 62, 73, 70, 68, 68, 64, 71 
Minibus: 51, 45, 59, 57, 58, 54, 46, 48 
Bus: 41, 48, 42, 39, 47, 49, 35, 41.
input.question: Calculate a one-way ANOVA on the above data and fill out an ANOVA table.

output.answer: Vehicle type: df = 2, Sums of squares = 2629.750, Mean squares = 1314.875, F = 58.516, P < 0.0001. Within: df = 21, Sums of squares = 471.875, Mean squares = 22.470. Total: df = 23, Sums of squares = 3101.625.

meta_info.chapter: Differences between more than two samples
meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
